sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.147 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: (V.\,M.\,Kopytov, N.\,Ya.\,Medvedev). A subgroup$H$of a right-orderable group~$G$is said to be right-relatively convex if it is convex under some right ordering on$G$. Is the lattice of right-relatively convex subgroups of a right-orderable group always a sublattice of the lattice of its subgroups?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
